wrangle
download

the Markdown editor built for AI-native development.

a native macOS workspace for developers who live in multi-agent workflows. edit your CLAUDE.md files, run agent sessions with embedded terminals, and never lose context switching between projects.

download — $9.99
product screenshot
terminal 
// the problem with your current setup

$ code system-prompt.md     // xml highlighting breaks
$ terminal-1: claude        // where was that session again?
$ terminal-2: gemini        // no context, no notifications
$ alt-tab alt-tab alt-tab   // death by window switching

$ wrangle .                 // ✓ everything in one place

// what you get

→ embedded terminals — run claude, gemini, and other agents in tabbed terminals. notifications pull you back when they need input or finish.
→ session context — see the linked claude file, active skills, and MCP servers for every running session at a glance.
→ token counting — always visible in the status bar. know your context usage before you hit send.
→ xml-in-markdown — highlights and collapses <tools>, <instructions>, <system> blocks
→ file awareness — distinct treatment for CLAUDE.md, SKILL.md, AGENTS.md
→ native macOS — swift, swiftui, apple silicon. not electron.
claude — project-api ● needs input 
$ claude
> analyzing src/api/routes.ts...
> found 3 issues in error handling
> ready for review. apply changes? (y/n)
CLAUDE.md 2 skills 3 MCP servers

// terminals & sessions

→ tabbed terminals multiple agent sessions running side by side. no more hunting for the right terminal window.
→ smart notifications get pulled back to the right tab when an agent needs a response or finishes its task.
→ session context every terminal shows its linked claude file, active skills, and connected MCP servers.
→ multi-project switch between projects without losing running sessions or terminal state.

if you're not running AI agents from your editor yet, you will be soon. wrangle was built by a developer who manages multiple claude and gemini sessions every day and got tired of context-switching between terminals, editors, and notification windows. one app. everything in view.

wrangle
